county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Carlisle. Formed 1799 from Bourbon and Mason. Northeastern Bluegrass. Nicholas County Historical Society. Blue Licks Battlefield State Resort Park (1782 battle). Carlisle community records. Covered bridge heritage. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Bourbon and Mason counties for pre-1799 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1799 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1799. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Nicholas County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1799.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

Tax lists naming slaveholders and number of enslaved; estate inventories naming enslaved individuals; slave schedules (1850, 1860); manumission records; bills of sale. Kentucky was a slave state that did not secede.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax lists and estate records generally well preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hartford. Formed 1798 from Hardin. Western Kentucky. Ohio County Historical Society. Hartford city records. Green River heritage. Rough River Lake area. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Hardin County for pre-1798 records. Parent county for several others including Butler and McLean.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1798 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1798. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1798.

source_profiles.source_name: Ohio County Coal Mining and Company Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Western Kentucky coalfield. Company town records, mine payrolls, commissary records, and United Mine Workers union records. Essential for documenting mining families.
Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in La Grange. Formed 1823 from Henry, Jefferson, and Shelby. North-central Kentucky near Louisville. Oldham County Historical Society. La Grange is known for trains running through Main Street. Rob Morris home (founder of Eastern Star). Kentucky Reformatory (1937) records. FamilySearch microfilm. Check parent counties for pre-1823 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1823 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1823. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1823.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1819 from Franklin, Gallatin, Pendleton, Scott.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Owenton. Formed 1819 from Franklin, Gallatin, Pendleton, and Scott. North-central Kentucky. Owen County Historical Society. Kentucky River area. Monterey community records. Eagle Station (Daniel Boone's last Kentucky home area). FamilySearch microfilm. Check four parent counties for pre-1819 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1819 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1819. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1819.

source_profiles.source_name: Owen County Coal Mining and Company Records
source_profiles.repository: Southeast KY Community & Technical College / UK Special Collections
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Eastern Kentucky coalfield. Company town records, mine payrolls, commissary records, and United Mine Workers union records. Essential for documenting mining families.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Booneville. 1929 courthouse fire destroyed most records. Formed 1843 from Breathitt, Clay, and Estill. Eastern Kentucky mountains. One of America's poorest counties historically — limited institutional resources. South Fork of Kentucky River. Owsley County Historical Society. KDLA has some surviving records. FamilySearch microfilm for post-1929 records. Check parent counties for pre-1843 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1929 to present. Some gaps due to 1929 courthouse fire destroyed most records.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1929. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Owsley County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1929.

source_profiles.repository: Southeast KY Community & Technical College / UK Special Collections
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Eastern Kentucky coalfield. Company town records, mine payrolls, commissary records, and United Mine Workers union records. Essential for documenting mining families.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Falmouth. Formed 1798 from Bracken and Campbell. Northern Kentucky. Pendleton County Historical Society. Falmouth and Licking River area — devastating floods documented in county records (1937, 1997). FamilySearch microfilm. Check Bracken and Campbell counties for pre-1798 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1798 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1798. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hazard. Formed 1820 from Clay and Floyd. Eastern Kentucky mountains. Perry County Historical Society. Hazard is a regional center for southeastern Kentucky. Coal mining central to county history — company town records. North Fork of Kentucky River. Bobby Davis Museum and Park. Lotts Creek Community School records. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1820. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1820.

source_profiles.source_name: Perry County Coal Mining and Company Records
source_profiles.repository: Southeast KY Community & Technical College / UK Special Collections
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Eastern Kentucky coalfield. Company town records, mine payrolls, commissary records, and United Mine Workers union records. Essential for documenting mining families.

Kentucky was a border state — both Union and Confederate service records. Union pension files at NARA; Confederate pension files at KDLA. Compiled Military Service Records for both sides. USCT (United States Colored Troops) records for African American soldiers.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Pikeville. Formed 1821 from Floyd. Kentucky's largest county by area. Eastern Kentucky mountains on West Virginia border. Pike County Historical Society. Pikeville is a regional center. Hatfield-McCoy feud territory — court records and depositions at KDLA and West Virginia archives. Coal mining extensive — coal baron and company records. Pikeville Cut-Through engineering records. University of Pikeville archives. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1821 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1821. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1821.

Kentucky was a border state — both Union and Confederate service records. Union pension files at NARA; Confederate pension files at KDLA. Compiled Military Service Records for both sides. USCT (United States Colored Troops) records for African American soldiers.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Stanton. Formed 1852 from Clark, Estill, and Montgomery. Eastern Kentucky foothills. Natural Bridge State Resort Park. Powell County Historical Society. Red River Gorge area. Lumber industry records — Kentucky Union Railway logging records. FamilySearch microfilm. Check parent counties for pre-1852 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1852 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1852. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Powell County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1852.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Somerset. Records well-preserved from 1798 formation. South-central Kentucky. Pulaski County Historical Society. Somerset is a major regional center with city directories. Lake Cumberland (Wolf Creek Dam, 1952) — displaced families records. Battle of Mill Springs/Logan's Cross Roads (1862) records. Pulaski was a strongly Unionist county. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1798 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1798. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).
